title: delphinium occidentale subsp. cucullatum
intro: Delphinium occidentale subsp. cucullatum, also known as western delphinium or mountain larkspur, is a perennial plant species that belongs to the buttercup family (Ranunculaceae). Traditionally, the plant has been used for its medicinal properties, particularly its roots, which contain delphinine, a toxic alkaloid with potential analgesic and anti-inflammatory effects. The health benefits of this plant include its use in treating fever, rheumatism, and other inflammatory conditions, although its toxicity requires careful handling and preparation. Herbal preparations of Delphinium occidentale subsp. cucullatum may include decoctions or infusions made from the roots or flowers, although their safe use and efficacy are not well-established.
